top_categories.workout_context: 11,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Sierra Nevada Límite Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 91 miles.
121ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Sierra Nevada Límite Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
At Sierra Nevada Límite Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Sierra Nevada Límite Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 91, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
11,000ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Sierra Nevada Límite Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 91 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Low-cadence force work for 11,000ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Sierra Nevada Límite Gran Fondo’s 91 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Sierra Nevada Límite Gran Fondo with 11,000ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Precipitation: Moderate, with possible flurries at elevation.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at Sierra Nevada Límite Gran Fondo demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride mixed terrain at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 91 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Gravel Specific, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 91 miles of mixed terrain in Cenes de la Vega, Granada, Spain.
